DISCOVERY, DEPT. 525, AS TO THE DEC-06-2024 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFFS DEBBIE GONZALEZ , BY AND THROUGH HER SUCCESSOR-IN-INTEREST, MAYACHERIE JACKSON, AND MAYACHERIE JACKSON'S MOTION TO QUASH AND/OR AMEND DEFENDANTS' DEPOSITION SUBPOENA FOR PRODUCTION OF MEDICAL RECORDS FROM UCSF MEDICAL CENTER; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR DETAILS). DENIED, WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO PLAINTIFF'S ABILITY TO RAISE OBJECTIONS TO PRODUCTION OF INFORMATION BEYOND THE SCOPE OF THE SUBPOENA(S), IN WHICH EVENT COUNSEL FOR THE PARTIES SHALL TIMELY MEET AND CONFER IN GOOD FAITH. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND EMAIL IT TO JUDGE PRO TEM AARON MINNIS AT AARON@MINNISANDSMALLETS.COM . JUDGE PRO TEM: AARON MINNIS; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (525/JPT)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-01-2025 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash And/Or Amend Defendants' Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Medicial Records From Ucsf Medical Center
Off Calendar Dec-06-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. THOMAS REYDA, Esq. of Dudensing Law (tel: 916-448-6400) for Plaintiffs.
Atty. BRENDA LIGORSKY, Esq. of Kelly, Trotter & Frazen (tel: 562-432-5855) for Defendants.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFFS DEBBIE GONZALEZ , BY AND THROUGH HER SUCCESSOR-IN-INTEREST, MAYACHERIE JACKSON, AND MAYACHERIE JACKSON's Motion To Quash And/Or Amend Defendants' Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Medical Records From Ucsf Medical Center.

Plaintiff contests the tentative ruling. Both parties stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem. The Court inquires counsel Thomas Reyda as to why he is not personally present in the Courtroom, as required for contesting parties. Counsel Thomas Reyda states he was unaware of the requirement and requests the Court to exercise its discretion and allow oral argument. Upon conclusion, the Court denies Counsel Thomas Reyda's request and does not allow counsel to argue the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Denied, without prejudice to Plaintiff's ability to raise objections to production of information beyond the scope of the subpoena(s), in which event counsel for the parties shall timely meet and confer in good faith.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and email it to Judge Pro Tem Aaron Minnis at aaron@minnisandsmallets.com .

Judge Pro Tem: Aaron Minnis; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (525/JPT)
